title: platform-user — subscribed events
description: Listeners this module registers against events published by other modules AND its own events. Every listener is discovered at boot via #[AsPlatformUserListener] where applicable, OR via the standard Laravel EventServiceProvider mapping for cross-module subscriptions.

listeners.purpose: When an Identity is soft-deleted (GDPR erasure hold started) AND a linked PlatformUser row exists AND that PlatformUser is not already offboarded, dispatch a synthetic offboarding lifecycle: fire PlatformUserOffboarded(trigger=identity_erased). Ensures a compliance-officer's identity-erasure request cascades to the staff plane cleanly. When the Identity's soft delete is hard-committed 30 days later (identity retention hold), the PlatformUser row has ALREADY been in retention hold and is 30 days into its own 90-day hold — the two schedules run concurrently.

When an Identity linked to a PlatformUser disables MFA, immediately: (1) write an audit row with severity=critical, (2) emit a `platform_user.mfa_coverage_ratio` gauge update, (3) dispatch a Slack notification to the security channel. Does NOT auto-suspend the PlatformUser — the `platform.mfa_required` middleware will refuse subsequent requests, and security decides whether to formally suspend.

When a linked Identity's password changes, revoke every Sanctum PAT on the platform_admin guard for the associated PlatformUser (defence in depth beyond the identity module's own session revocation). Ensures a compromised password does not leak an unclosed platform-admin session.

Synchronous dispatcher — pushes RevokePlatformUserAccessJob to the notifications-critical queue in the same request cycle so the 24h SLA clock starts immediately. Cannot itself be queued (a queue backlog would defeat the SLA measurement).

Sends PlatformUserOffboardingScheduledNotification to the offboarded staffer's manager + the ops distribution list + security. Includes the ACL-inventory report link (what tenants + tools this PlatformUser had access to).
Sends PlatformUserHiredNotification. Includes onboarding-checklist link + department Slack channel invite + first-day agenda link.

For every active impersonation session where this PlatformUser is the impersonator, transition ended_at=NULL, paused_at=NOW() (Wave 1b impersonation table shape) — pauses but does NOT terminate the session. On PlatformUserRestored, sessions must be manually resumed by the impersonator (does NOT auto-resume; defence against a rushed restore leaking access).

Purge the `platform-org-chart` cache tag (`platform-user:reports:*`, `platform-user:manager-chain:*`) so subsequent reads pick up the new topology. Synchronous so subsequent requests in the same batch see fresh data.

On every fresh platform_admin login, update the `Identity.last_login_at` (indirectly via the identity module's own listener). Does NOT stamp `PlatformUser.last_login_at` — that column does not exist on platform_users; the identity.last_login_at is the source of truth. Optional debouncing to batch writes across 5-minute windows.

When an audit row is written on the platform plane, propagate the correlation_id from the request context. Cheap listener — a single UPDATE to set correlation_id on the audit row. Enables the `platform-user:audit-report` command to group related rows by correlation id.
